video_title: How To Use The New Netflix Experience
comment_text: In mid-June, when I got this abomination on my Google TV, I advised in a comment to this video that instead of cancelling, people can just uninstall the app update and reset it to version 9.0.0 or whatever version got shipped with their tv. This is what I did back then, and I got the old UI back this way. It was obvious this wouldn't be a long-term solution, but I thought the way it would end would be that the Netflix service would stop communicating with the client app at some point. Instead, Netflix actually had the audacity to change the way their service communicates with old versions of the client as well, so now version 9.0.0 displays the awful new UI as well. Reverting to an outdated version of the client (at least this one) doesn't help anymore. Okay, Netflix, you go this extra mile to f@ck with your subscribers, I'll be happy to oblige and f@ck you right back. I have cancelled my subscription and won't be coming back even if you should decide to give your users the option to select the UI that they prefer.

video_title: How To Use The New Netflix Experience
comment_text:
Yea this is awful. 

The panels are too big for absolutely no reason now. Clicking through titles feels nauseating with how choppy it is. Scrolling down the home page gives off the impression that Netflix reeeeeally wants you to click on the first option in each menu section, with how big the preview pane are. It's just too much.

There is no longer a categories tab. This essentially limits the users ability to browse (part of the actual experience of CHOOSING what to watch). In fact, the whole experience of using the new UI feels less like picking from a menu and more like being handed a plate of food and told "yea sorry that's all we've got. eat up." Netflix knows that they're supposed to be offering an ENTERTAINMENT SERVICE, right?? Kind of feels like they've let the ball drop on that front in recent years 

I agree with other commentors that the option to customise the layout (and size of preview boxes!!!) would be a better upgrade than this. Let people choose the sections they want to show up on their home page, and in what order. Hell, give people unique skins for the app based on what shows they've watched. Reward long-term viewership with discounts or limited edition profile pics. Make using your service feel enjoyable for a change.

Can't shake the feeling that we're going to see a depressing AI announcement any day now that'll ruin the site for good.

video_title: How To Use The New Netflix Experience
comment_text:
Horrendous design.  Extremely disappointing coming a pioneer in streaming with the experience that you would expect not to fumble this badly (Qwikster/New Coke level blunder).  The old layout just worked well, and I would say the best out of the major streaming apps.  The app was a saving grace considering their content has fizzled as of late.  Now Netflix is at the bottom app heap along with Apple TV+, which they inexplicably seem to be emulating.  It now takes so much longer to scroll through shows/movies and My List.  The old app's use of screen real estate and My List grid was SO much more efficient.  Everyone I talked to hates the new interface.  My mind is blown that they could have had a focus group in which the majority liked this new interface – where did you find these people??

Give an option to revert or I’m finishing up a couple series and canceling.

video_title: How To Use The New Netflix Experience
comment_text:
Seriously, Netflix feels like a graveyard of cancelled shows these days. I honestly don’t even know why I’m still subscribed—I can’t remember the last time I actually watched anything on it. Lately, I’ve been way more into Prime Video. They’re adapting so many amazing books into series and films, and I’m genuinely excited about what they’re doing.
Sure, Netflix is also trying to adapt content, but the quality often just doesn’t measure up. Take *Damsel* with Millie Bobby Brown, for example—the dragon looked like it was pulled straight out of a 25-year-old TV movie. It’s hard to take these productions seriously when the CGI is so underwhelming.
Another thing: Netflix seems to cram excessive sex scenes into everything, leaving little room for real storytelling. And their recent releases? Most are plagued by bad CGI, weak narratives, and nothing genuinely exciting on the horizon.
Don’t even get me started on Netflix India. It’s practically run by nepotism and celebrity influence. Despite having massive budgets, the content they churn out often feels cheap and uninspired. And where is *Baahubali: Before the Beginning*? We've been waiting nearly eight years! If it were Prime, I’m pretty sure we’d have watched the final season by now.
